financial_performance: Americas net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
Europe net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
Greater China net sales increased during Q1 2026 due to higher iPhone net sales.
Japan net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher iPhone and iPad net sales.
Rest of Asia Pacific net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
iPhone net sales increased during Q1 2026 due to higher net sales of Pro models.
Mac net sales decreased during Q1 2026 primarily due to lower net sales of laptops and desktops.
iPad net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher iPad and iPad Pro net sales.
Wearables, Home and Accessories net sales decreased during Q1 2026 primarily due to lower Wearables net sales.
Services net sales increased during Q1 2026 primarily due to higher advertising, App Store and cloud services net sales.
Products gross margin increased during Q1 2026 due to different product mix, partially offset by tariff costs.
Services gross margin increased during Q1 2026 due to higher Services net sales and different service mix.
R&D expense growth during Q1 2026 was driven by increases in infrastructure costs, headcount expenses and engineering program costs.
Selling, general and administrative expense growth during Q1 2026 was driven by increases in headcount and variable selling expenses.
Effective tax rate for Q1 2026 was higher compared to Q1 2025 due to foreign currency loss regulations.
guidance_and_forward_outlook: Company intends to increase its dividend on an annual basis, subject to Board of Directors declaration.
Company believes cash balances and operations will satisfy cash requirements and capital return program over next 12 months.
Company believes gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.
material_events: Company announced updated 14-inch MacBook Pro during Q1 2026.
Company announced updated iPad Pro during Q1 2026.
Company announced updated Apple Vision Pro during Q1 2026.
2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders was held on February 24, 2026.
Directors were elected at Annual Meeting to serve until next annual meeting.
Management proposal to ratify Ernst & Young LLP as independent registered public accounting firm was approved.
Advisory resolution to approve executive compensation was approved.
Management proposal to approve Non-Employee Director Stock Plan was approved.
Shareholder proposal entitled 'China Entanglement Audit' was not approved.
risk_factors_emphasized: There have been no material changes to the Company's risk factors since the 2025 Form 10-K.
explicit_uncertainties: Ultimate impact of tariffs remains uncertain and will depend on several factors including additional measures.
Forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance and actual results may differ significantly.
Company assumes no obligation to revise forward-looking statements except as required by law.
Company is currently evaluating the timing and method of adoption of ASU 2025-06.
notable_causal_statements: Americas net sales increased due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
Europe net sales increased due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
Greater China net sales increased due to higher iPhone net sales.
Japan net sales increased due to higher iPhone and iPad net sales.
Rest of Asia Pacific net sales increased due to higher iPhone and Services net sales.
iPhone net sales increased due to higher Pro model net sales.
Mac net sales decreased due to lower laptop and desktop net sales.
iPad net sales increased due to higher iPad and iPad Pro net sales, partially offset by lower iPad mini.
Wearables net sales decreased due to lower Wearables net sales.
Services net sales increased due to higher advertising, App Store and cloud services net sales.
Products gross margin increased due to different product mix, partially offset by tariff costs.
Services gross margin increased due to higher Services net sales and different service mix.
R&D expense growth was driven by infrastructure costs, headcount expenses and engineering program costs increases.
Effective tax rate was higher due to foreign currency loss regulations and foreign currency revaluations impact.
